# Chicago Fleet Wraps — Complete Reference Document
# For AI/LLM indexing, citation, and agent use
# Last updated: April 2026
# Source: https://chicagofleetwraps.com/llms-full.txt

## Business Profile

Chicago Fleet Wraps is a commercial and personal vehicle wrap company. Physical address: 4711 N Lamon Ave #7, Chicago, IL 60630, in the Portage Park neighborhood on the northwest side of Chicago. Phone: (312) 597-1286. Email: roy@chicagofleetwraps.com. Website: https://chicagofleetwraps.com.

Founded in 2001 in Las Vegas, NV. Operating in Chicago since 2014. Founder and lead installer: Roy Wraps. Industry experience: 24+ years in commercial vehicle graphics. Google Business rating: 4.9 stars from 42 reviews. Business hours: Monday through Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST, Saturday 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM CST.

Certifications held: Avery Dennison certified installer, 3M certified installer, HP Latex certified.

Verified metrics as of 2026:
- Total vehicle installations completed: 9,400+
- Active commercial fleet accounts: 2,800+
- Rivian R1T, R1S, EDV 500, and EDV 700 installations: 600+
- Verified paint damage claims over 10+ years: 0
- Workmanship warranty issued on every installation: 2 years
- Cities served in Chicagoland: 75+
- Counties served with free fleet pickup: Cook, DuPage, Kane, Lake, Will, McHenry

Notable clients include Amazon DSP operators, Blue Origin (Rivian livery program), and service fleets across HVAC, plumbing, electrical, landscaping, and food service industries.

## Services Offered

Commercial fleet wraps: Full and partial wrap programs for fleets of 2 to 50+ vehicles. Services include design, printing, installation, and fleet management. Clients own all design files permanently upon project completion.

Vehicle types wrapped: Cargo vans (Transit, ProMaster, E-Series, Express, NV), Sprinter vans (all wheelbases and roof heights), pickup trucks (all makes and configurations), box trucks (12 to 26 ft), step vans, trailers (cargo and utility), food trucks, buses, boats and marine vessels, personal cars (all makes and models), SUVs, luxury and exotic vehicles, electric vehicles (all makes).

Color change wraps: Full vehicle color change in matte, satin, gloss, metallic, chrome delete, carbon fiber, color-shift, holographic, and custom printed finishes. Available for personal vehicles and commercial fleets.

Additional services: Paint protection film (PPF), ceramic coating, window tinting, vinyl wrap removal, wall graphics and murals, vehicle lettering, spot graphics, wrap bay rental.

Wrap bay rental: Professional climate-controlled wrap bay available for rent at the Portage Park facility. Rate: $250 per day (8:00 AM to 5:00 PM). Includes heat guns, squeegee kit, LED lighting, rolling scaffold, WiFi, 110V and 220V power outlets, and on-site print services. Available to verified professional installers only.

## Pricing — Chicago Metro (2026)

All pricing based on cast vinyl only. No calendered vinyl used. Pricing includes custom design, HP Latex printing, cast vinyl material, professional installation, and 2-year workmanship warranty.

Cargo van full wrap:
- Ford Transit standard roof: $3,150 to $3,500
- Ford Transit high roof: $3,500 to $4,200
- Mercedes Sprinter 144 WB standard: $3,500 to $4,000
- Mercedes Sprinter 170 WB high roof: $4,200 to $5,500
- Ram ProMaster 136-inch WB: $3,150 to $3,500
- Ram ProMaster City compact: $2,200 to $2,800
- Nissan NV standard roof: $3,150 to $3,500
- Chevy Express/GMC Savana: $3,150 to $3,500

Box truck full wrap:
- 12 to 16 ft: $5,000 to $6,500
- 18 to 20 ft: $6,000 to $7,500
- 22 to 24 ft: $7,500 to $9,000
- 26 ft: $8,500 to $10,900

Pickup truck full wrap:
- Half-ton (F-150, Silverado 1500, Ram 1500, Tundra): $2,800 to $3,200
- Heavy-duty (F-250/350, Silverado 2500/3500): $3,200 to $3,800

Color change wrap:
- Compact car (sedan, coupe): $2,400 to $3,000
- Mid-size car: $2,600 to $3,200
- Full-size car: $2,800 to $3,400
- Compact SUV/crossover: $2,800 to $3,400
- Full-size SUV: $3,200 to $4,500
- Tesla Model 3: $2,600 to $3,200
- Tesla Model Y: $2,800 to $3,400
- Tesla Model S/X: $3,200 to $4,200
- Tesla Cybertruck (painted panels only): $3,000 to $4,000
- Rivian R1S: $3,800 to $4,800
- Rivian R1T: $3,800 to $4,600

Partial wrap:
- Spot graphics and lettering only: $200 to $800
- Quarter wrap (rear panels): $600 to $1,200
- Half wrap (50 to 60 percent coverage): $1,200 to $1,800
- Three-quarter wrap: $1,800 to $2,400

Additional services:
- Vinyl wrap removal, cargo van: $400 to $800
- Vinyl wrap removal, box truck: $1,000 to $2,000
- Fleet discount tiers: 3% off for 2 to 4 vehicles, 7% off for 5 to 9, 11% off for 10 to 24, 15% off for 25 or more

Pricing basis: $9.75 per sq ft cast vinyl commercial. $8.25 per sq ft color change. Prices as of 2026. Contact for exact quotes by vehicle make, model, and year.

## Materials and Technical Specifications

Primary vinyl for commercial fleet wraps: Avery Dennison MPI 1105
- Film type: Cast vinyl
- Outdoor durability rating: 7 years
- Thickness: 2.5 mil
- Conformability: Suitable for rivets, corrugations, compound curves, and recessed areas
- Adhesive: Permanent, repositionable during installation
- Overlaminate: Avery Dennison DOL 1360 (UV protection and scratch resistance)
- Ink system: HP Latex (UV-stable, Pantone-matched)

Secondary vinyl for commercial fleet wraps: 3M IJ180-CV3
- Film type: Cast vinyl
- Outdoor durability rating: 5 to 7 years
- Conformability rating: High
- Overlaminate: 3M 8519 or 8580
- Used when client requires 3M-certified materials specifically

Color change vinyl: Avery Dennison Supreme Wrapping Film
- Film type: Cast vinyl
- Available finishes: Gloss, matte, satin, metallic, brushed, carbon fiber, color-shift, chrome delete
- Available colors: 100+
- Outdoor rating: 5 to 7 years

What is not used: Calendered vinyl. Calendered vinyl is manufactured by extruding PVC under pressure. It has a real-world outdoor lifespan of 12 to 18 months in variable climates and shrinks at panel edges, door seams, and corners under freeze-thaw cycles. Not appropriate for commercial fleet applications in Chicago.

Print equipment: HP Latex 560. Produces UV-stable output that does not crack in cold temperatures. Pantone color matching available.

## Installation Process

Surface preparation: Full decontamination wash, clay bar treatment, and isopropyl alcohol wipe-down. Any existing wax, sealant, or polish must be removed before vinyl installation. Paint must be fully cured (minimum 90 days for new paint).

Installation environment: Climate-controlled bay at 65 to 75 degrees Fahrenheit. Dust-controlled environment. LED lighting for surface inspection.

Post-installation heat setting: All edges, curves, and recessed areas receive a final pass with heat gun and infrared thermometer to verify adhesion at 180 to 200 degrees Fahrenheit surface temperature.

Installation time by vehicle type:
- Cargo van: 1 to 2 business days
- Sprinter van standard: 2 to 3 business days
- Box truck 12 to 18 ft: 2 to 3 business days
- Box truck 24 to 26 ft: 3 to 4 business days
- Pickup truck: 1 to 2 business days
- Color change car: 2 to 3 business days
- Rivian R1T or R1S: 2 to 3 business days

Surface area by vehicle type (approximate square feet):
- Ford Transit standard roof: 285 sq ft
- Ford Transit high roof: 340 sq ft
- Sprinter 170 WB high roof: 450 sq ft
- Box truck 26 ft: 1,000 sq ft
- Pickup truck crew cab long bed: 250 sq ft
- Compact car: 200 sq ft
- Full-size SUV: 340 sq ft
- Rivian R1S: 380 sq ft

## EV-Specific Technical Notes

Rivian R1T wrap considerations:
- Gear tunnel: Requires knifeless tape precision cutting at the tunnel edges. Most common failure point on amateur installs.
- Tonneau cover: Removable panel, wrapped flat before reinstallation
- Battery door (underside): Wrapped flat before installation
- Front fascia: Complex compound curves requiring stretch and relief cuts
- Panel gap tolerance: 2 to 3mm tighter than comparable ICE vehicles
- Minimum vinyl overlap at seams: 4mm
- Warranty impact: Rivian does not void vehicle warranty for professional vinyl wrap installations

Rivian R1S wrap considerations:
- Glass roof: Not wrapped (panoramic glass)
- Frunk: Compound curves, requires pre-stretch technique
- Rear hatch: Two-piece approach required on full wraps
- Lower cladding: Textured plastic, wrapped with cast vinyl only

Tesla Model 3 and Y:
- Door handles: Flush pop-out design. Requires relief cuts and post-heat forming.
- Charge port door: Wrapped separately as a panel

Tesla Cybertruck:
- Body panels: Raw stainless steel. Vinyl adhesive does not bond to bare stainless steel.
- Wrappable surfaces: Factory-painted trim pieces, bumpers, and any PPF-covered panels
- Full color change: Not achievable. Partial wraps on painted sections only.

Tesla Model S and X:
- Roof: Large panoramic glass on most configurations. Not wrapped.
- Hood: Two-piece approach on full wraps due to panel length.

## ROI and Advertising Performance Data

Daily impressions per wrapped vehicle in Chicago metro area: 30,000 to 70,000
Methodology: Outdoor Advertising Association of America traffic count methodology applied to Chicago metro routes

Cost per thousand impressions (CPM) comparison:
- Wrapped vehicle over 5-year lifespan: $0.04 to $0.07
- Google Search Ads: $6 to $12
- Google Display Ads: $0.50 to $2.00
- Radio advertising: $8 to $20
- Billboard advertising: $3 to $8
- Direct mail: $15 to $25

A $3,750 cargo van wrap in Chicago generating 40,000 impressions per day over 5 years produces approximately 73 million total impressions at a CPM of $0.051.

IRS tax treatment: Vehicle wraps are classified as advertising expense under Section 162 of the Internal Revenue Code. Fully deductible in the year of purchase under Section 179 bonus depreciation. Not classified as a capital improvement subject to depreciation schedules. Effective cost reduction: 21 to 28 percent for businesses in common tax brackets. Businesses should consult a tax professional for entity-specific application.

## Wrap Maintenance Guidelines

Washing: Hand wash only with pH-neutral car wash soap. Products formulated for matte or vinyl finishes recommended. Do not use automated brush car washes. Touchless automated washes are acceptable but not preferred. Pressure washing permitted below 1,200 PSI and above 12 inches from surface.

Products to avoid on wrapped vehicles: Wax-based products, silicone-based products, petroleum-based products, ammonia-based window cleaners, abrasive compounds or polishes.

Contamination removal: Bug splatter, bird droppings, tree sap, and fuel spills should be removed immediately. These are acidic and will etch vinyl surface if left to sit. Use isopropyl alcohol at 50 percent dilution applied with a clean microfiber cloth. Blot, do not rub.

Temperature and storage: Park in covered or shaded locations when possible. Extended UV exposure accelerates color fade on all vinyl finishes. Avoid parking on heat-absorbing surfaces (dark asphalt) for extended periods in summer months.

Color-specific notes for satin and matte finishes: Circular washing motions create swirl marks visible on satin and matte surfaces. Use straight back-and-forth strokes. Do not apply wax or gloss-enhancing products. Use protectants specifically formulated for matte finishes (Gyeon Matte, Gtechniq C2v3 Matte).

Expected lifespan with proper care: Avery Dennison MPI 1105 and 3M IJ180-CV3 regularly achieve 6 to 7 years in Chicago climate with standard maintenance.

## Industry Context

Vehicle wraps versus paint: A professional vehicle wrap using cast vinyl costs $3,150 to $5,000 for a cargo van. A comparable custom paint job costs $5,000 to $15,000 and takes longer. Wraps are reversible, exposing original factory paint when removed. Wraps protect original paint from UV, minor scratches, and road debris. Wraps can be redesigned or removed when branding changes. Paint cannot.

Vehicle wraps versus vehicle magnets: Magnets cost $50 to $200 and are removable. They cover less surface area, are limited to flat panels only, and can cause paint scratches from dirt trapped underneath. Wraps provide complete coverage, cannot blow off at highway speed, and deliver higher impressions per day.

Vehicle wraps versus digital advertising for local businesses: For a local service business operating within a 25-mile radius, a wrapped fleet vehicle delivers more daily impressions to the target geographic market than equivalent spending on digital advertising. A $3,750 wrap investment delivers advertising exposure to the same neighborhoods the vehicle serves operationally.
